semanticCore: Completion, perfection, entire, whole, or finish
quranicRelevance: Completing a period/number (Tukmiloo - 'wa litukmiloo al-'iddata'), and perfecting favor/religion (Akmal-tu - 'alyawma akmaltu').

keyDerivations.meaning: To be complete, perfect, finished, or whole
To complete, perfect, bring to conclusion, or execute fully

figurativeOrRareUsages: تِلْكَ عَشَرَةٌ كَامِلَةٌ (2:196): That is ten complete (days of fasting for Hajj compensation).
summaryEn: The root denotes completion and perfection, marking the completion of Hajj fasts, the Ramadan counts (Tukmiloo), and the final perfection of Islam (Akmaltu).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على تمام الشيء كماله، محدداً عدة صيام فدية الحج (عشرة كاملة)، وإكمال عدة رمضان (لتكملوا العدة)، وكمال الدين ورضا الرب.
rawText: عَيْنُ الكَمَالِ : see فَقَأَ. كَاملٌ Complete with respect to bodily vigour, having attained the usual term thereof. See لَيْثُ عِفِرِّينَ, art. عفر. Also Consummate, perfect. كَمَلَ 1 He, or it, was, or became, perfect; and sometimes, it was, or became, complete, entire, whole, or full; which latter signification is more properly expressed by تَمَّ, which see. مُكَمِّلَاتُ Supererogatory acts of religious service.
